emit._note: unlockData = abi.encode(bytes actions, bytes[] params). The dispatcher pre-decodes it into (commands, inputs[]) — structurally identical to the Universal Router envelope — then runs the shared per-opcode dispatch. V4 actions are NOT masked (full byte 0x00-0x1b), so mask=0xff / allow_revert_bit=0x00. Settlement actions (0x0b-0x16: SETTLE/SETTLE_PAIR/TAKE/TAKE_PAIR/CLOSE_CURRENCY/CLEAR_OR_TAKE/SWEEP/WRAP/UNWRAP) are absent from per_opcode_body + policy=skip → silently dropped: the liquidity action IS the intent, and they would otherwise add Unknown noise that trips the policy engine's warn-on-Unknown default. Swap codes (0x06-0x09) are UnsupportedAction inside modifyLiquidities (PositionManager table only) and are likewise skipped. Native-ETH V4 pools (currency0 == address(0)) currently surface the native leg as erc20{0x0...0}; a value-map native-token mapping is a follow-up. pool_manager is injected per-chain by the route handler as $resolved.pool_manager (mainnet/Base/OP/ARB V4 PoolManager singletons).

0x00.name: INCREASE_LIQUIDITY
0x00.inputs_abi: (uint256 tokenId, uint256 liquidity, uint128 amount0Max, uint128 amount1Max, bytes hookData)
0x00._note: INCREASE carries only tokenId — PoolKey is looked up on-chain via positionInfo[tokenId], so pool_id/hooks are NOT statically recoverable (D9). pool_id = 'unknown' sentinel; hooks = zero address. amount0Max/amount1Max are slippage UPPER bounds (validateMaxIn). amount_min is unknown statically → '0'.

0x01.name: DECREASE_LIQUIDITY
0x01.inputs_abi: (uint256 tokenId, uint256 liquidity, uint128 amount0Min, uint128 amount1Min, bytes hookData)
0x01._note: DECREASE shares INCREASE's wire decoder (uint256 tokenId, uint256 liquidity, uint128, uint128, bytes); slots 2/3 are slippage LOWER bounds amount0Min/amount1Min (validateMinOut). liquidity is uint256 on the wire but bound into the U128 liquidity_burn (fits for every real position; > u128 would reject at deserialize, mirroring V3). pool_id NOT statically recoverable → 'unknown'.

0x02.name: MINT_POSITION
0x02.inputs_abi: (address currency0, address currency1, uint24 fee, int24 tickSpacing, address hooks, int24 tickLower, int24 tickUpper, uint256 liquidity, uint128 amount0Max, uint128 amount1Max, address owner, bytes hookData)
0x02._note: MINT_POSITION is the only modifyLiquidities action fully self-describing from calldata: the PoolKey is encoded INLINE (head-flattened 5 fields: currency0, currency1, fee, tickSpacing, hooks) at the head of params[i], so the inputs_abi is a FLAT 12-field tuple (no nested offset). pool_id = keccak256(abi.encode(poolKey)) is computed in Rust by the dispatcher and injected as the synthetic $inputs.pool_id (the manifest can't hash). hooks/currency0/currency1 come straight from the inline PoolKey. owner is the recipient (address(1)=MSG_SENDER / address(2)=ADDRESS_THIS sentinels surface verbatim — sentinel→self mapping is a follow-up). amount0Max/amount1Max are slippage upper bounds. range.liquidity placeholder '0' (the position's minted liquidity is on-chain-assigned).

0x03.name: BURN_POSITION
0x03.inputs_abi: (uint256 tokenId, uint128 amount0Min, uint128 amount1Min, bytes hookData)
0x03._note: BURN_POSITION auto-decreases remaining liquidity to 0 then burns the position NFT. Only tokenId in calldata → pool_id NOT statically recoverable → 'unknown'. amount0Min/amount1Min are slippage lower bounds (not surfaced — concentrated_burn carries no amount field).

0x04.name: INCREASE_LIQUIDITY_FROM_DELTAS
0x04._note: DEPRECATED (Actions.sol @notice: 'Vulnerable to sandwich attacks - do not use'). Emitted as Unknown so the policy engine can warn/deny rather than treat it as a normal add. Params intentionally NOT decoded (the intent signal is 'a deprecated, slippage-unprotected liquidity action was used').

0x05.name: MINT_POSITION_FROM_DELTAS
0x05._note: DEPRECATED (Actions.sol @notice: 'Vulnerable to sandwich attacks - do not use'). Emitted as Unknown (same rationale as 0x04).
